disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Heavy coffee consumption today, considerably more than usual intake
trigger_question_examples: Have you had much caffeine today — coffee, energy drinks, tea?
Tell me about what you've had to eat or drink today.
How much coffee would you say you've had today compared to a usual day?
Any stimulants — coffee, energy drinks — today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, honestly I've had a lot of coffee today, way more than I normally would — I've been trying to push through long work hours.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: Just my normal coffee, I think.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Absence of true syncope, exertional chest pain, or resting shortness of breath; only brief lightheadedness accompanying the heavy beat
trigger_question_examples: Have you actually fainted or blacked out at any point?
Any chest pain, especially with exertion, or shortness of breath at rest?
When you feel lightheaded, has it ever made you pass out?
Do you get breathless or chest pain when this happens?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't fainted — I get a brief lightheaded feeling with the big thud, but it passes. No real chest pain and I'm not short of breath just sitting here.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I feel a bit odd but I can't really tell, I'm so anxious.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long working hours, heavy fatigue, and sleep deprivation preceding onset
trigger_question_examples: How have your work and sleep been lately?
Have you been particularly tired or run-down recently?
Is there a lot going on for you at the moment?
How much rest have you been getting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been working really long hours and I'm completely exhausted — not sleeping enough at all lately.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No family history of sudden cardiac death, inherited heart conditions, or early cardiac disease
trigger_question_examples: Is there any family history of heart problems, especially sudden or unexplained deaths at a young age?
Has anyone in your family died suddenly and unexpectedly or had inherited heart conditions?
Any family history of heart disease that you're aware of?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, not that I know of — no one in my family has had heart problems or died suddenly or anything like that.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm not really sure, I'd have to ask my family.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is my heart failing? It feels like it keeps stopping. Please just tell me if this is serious, I'm terrified.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Respond with genuine empathy and validation of the fear without dismissing it, while calmly explaining that the sensation of the heart 'stopping' followed by a strong beat is typically an extra/skipped beat rather than the heart failing, pending a full history. Should not deliver a flat purely clinical reassurance without acknowledging the distress, and should not over-promise certainty before completing the assessment.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Should I go to the emergency department tonight? I don't want to ignore this if something is really wrong.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain clearly why emergency attendance is not indicated tonight given the reassuring history (no true syncope, no exertional chest pain, no resting breathlessness, likely caffeine/fatigue trigger), while validating the concern, providing clear safety-netting for what would change that assessment, and arranging a routine follow-up ECG rather than leaving the patient with no plan.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient repeatedly circles back to the fear that his heart is stopping or failing rather than answering the specific question asked — tests whether the AI Doctor can gently redirect to gather history without dismissing the anxiety.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: I just keep feeling like my heart's going to stop... sorry, what did you ask? Right — yeah, it definitely feels worse when I'm lying still in bed.
